fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Wyoming Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Wyoming - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity wyoming, how to transfer audiologist license to wyoming, audiologist compact wyoming, wyoming audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Speech Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Wyoming Mental Health Professions Licensing Board
board.mailing_address:
Wyoming Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
Professional Licensing Boards
2020 Carey Avenue, Suite 200
Cheyenne, WY 82002

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Wyoming.

steps.title: Register for an Account
steps.description: Navigate to the Wyoming eGov Licenses Portal (wybop.license.pro.hps.state.wy.us) and click 'Register' to create a new user account if you do not already have one. Complete the registration process by providing all required personal information.

steps.title: Start New Application
steps.description: Log in to your account on the Wyoming eGov Licenses Portal, navigate to the 'Apply for a License' section, and select the Audiologist application form to begin.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Complete the online application form and upload all necessary supporting documents, including official transcripts verifying a qualifying degree in audiology, verification of supervised clinical practice (e.g., Clinical Fellowship Year report), and official verification of a passing score on the national examination (Praxis).

steps.title: Pass Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Successfully complete the Wyoming State Jurisprudence Examination, which is typically administered online as part of the application process within the Wyoming eGov Licenses Portal.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable initial Audiologist license application fee of $250.00 through the Wyoming eGov Licenses Portal using an accepted payment method.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Issuance
steps.description: After submitting all required items, the Board will review your application. Once approved, your Audiologist license will be issued. You can check the status of your application through the Wyoming eGov Licenses Portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Wyoming audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Wyoming have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Wyoming participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Wyoming without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Wyoming?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Wyoming while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

official_links.registration_steps: Go to the Wyoming eGov Licenses Portal, click 'Register' for new users, and follow the prompts to create an account. Once registered, log in and select 'Apply for a License' for Audiologists.
